title: Derivatives
description: GET /derivatives?inode=N — the derivative MANIFEST for one asset: which machine-derived artifacts exist (thumbnail/filmstrip/waveform/proxy/tech/embedding/transcript/ocr/faces), their status, integrity hash, producer, and (for blob kinds) the volume-relative blob path. JM-14, the Tier-B foundation. CLIENT-AGNOSTIC: OpenLoupe (Swift) and the JuiceMount web UI consume this identically. Keyed by the durable JuiceFS inode (from /lookup), never the mount path. Machine-derived data is a rebuildable cache — the media is the truth — so a client that distrusts a derivative (hash mismatch) just regenerates it. PROXY-CODEC additive fields (PROXY_CODEC_SPEC §JuiceMount ratification, schema version 2): proxy rows MAY carry `codec` (h264 default/floor | hevc | av1; absent ⇒ h264), `codec_string` (the exact isTypeSupported/canPlayType token), and `blob_size` (actual produced bytes). All additive — OL feature-detects on field presence, so old/new clients interoperate with no contract_version bump.

inode.description: Echo of the queried JuiceFS inode.

exists.description: Is the source asset present in the metadata mirror.

source_hash.description: Content hash (xxh3 hex) of the CURRENT source. Clients compare each derivative's `hash` against this and FAIL CLOSED (drop + regenerate) on mismatch. null when not yet computed.

derivatives.description: One entry per derivative kind known for this asset. Absent kinds may be omitted or listed with status:absent.

kind.description: Blob kinds (thumbnail/filmstrip/waveform/proxy/ai) carry blob_rel_path; DB kinds (tech/embedding/transcript/ocr/faces) are fetched via /metadata. 'ai' is a JSON blob (LoupeJSON `ai` shape: embeddings/faces/transcript/auto-tags) — JM-17.

producer.description: Which lane produced it. linux-farm = the portable NAS fast-lane; macos-node/on-device = Apple-framework AI.

hash.description: xxh3 of the SOURCE bytes this derivative was produced from. Client trusts the derivative only if hash == source_hash (fail-closed).

blob_rel_path.description: Volume-relative path under /.juicemount/derivatives/<inode>/ for blob kinds (e.g. "poster.jpg", "strip.jpg", "proxy.mp4"). null for DB-only kinds. The Mac client reads it through the mount; the web UI serves the same bytes over HTTP (byte-range-capable: Accept-Ranges + 206).

media_type.description: MIME of the blob (web-native by design: image/jpeg, image/webp, application/json for waveforms, video/mp4 fMP4/HLS for proxies). null for DB kinds.

model.description: Embedding model id+version for kind=embedding/faces/ai (so kNN partitions by model). null otherwise.

updated_at.description: Unix seconds the derivative was last produced.

source_size.description: Source file size (bytes) at generation time — lets a consumer stat-verify the derivative off the manifest (live /lookup size == source_size) without a separate tech row. Omitted on rows that predate this field.

source_mtime.description: Source file mtime (unix seconds) at generation time; the companion stat-guard to source_size.

codec.description: PROXY-CODEC (PROXY_CODEC_SPEC §JuiceMount ratification §2). Which video codec THIS proxy blob is. Present only on kind=='proxy'. ABSENT ⇒ assume "h264" (back-compat: every pre-existing proxy is the H.264 floor). H.264 is the guaranteed-decodable floor; the farm MUST NOT emit an asset whose only proxy is non-h264. Invariant across all codecs: MP4 container, +faststart, closed-GOP/CFR/BT.709 discipline, byte-range serving.

codec_string.description: PROXY-CODEC. The exact MediaSource.isTypeSupported()/canPlayType() / AVURLAsset probe token for THIS blob (e.g. "avc1.640028, mp4a.40.2"). The web UI and OpenLoupe MUST gate on this, not on `codec` alone. Present only on kind=='proxy'.

blob_size.description: PROXY-CODEC / REMOTE_PROXY (IN-FLIGHT-1). The actual produced size of the blob in bytes (os.Stat of the blob at generation time). Required-intent on kind=='proxy'; permitted (additive) on all blob kinds. OpenLoupe OBSERVES this and owns the downgrade/skip/cap policy (skip the proxy when blob_size >= original.size; quantify the saving). JuiceMount makes NO size-ratio guarantee — only honesty: blob_size reflects the real blob.

filmstrip.description: Sprite-sheet geometry — present ONLY when kind=='filmstrip' (JM-16). Lets a scrubber map a time to a cell rect: i = round(t_ms/interval_ms); row = i/cols; col = i%cols; rect = (col*cell_w, row*cell_h, cell_w, cell_h). Cells fill row-major, left-to-right, top-to-bottom.

frame_count.description: Total frames in the sheet (<= cols*rows; trailing cells in the last row may be blank).

interval_ms.description: Wall-clock spacing between consecutive frames; frame i ~= i*interval_ms.
